behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-API-adapter handoff: taxonomy must survive request construction and be present in outbound payload (MOCK-MISS). In-process stub transport can catch missing/partial labels; real OS/network is not required (REAL-INFRA no). Correctness does not depend on model judgment (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is about request contents, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload; invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert request contains the full label set exactly once before send.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at response handoff: API response parsing + taxonomy validation + module return contract across modules (MOCK-MISS). A stubbed API response with invalid label is sufficient; no real OS/network needed (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model output quality, only defensive mapping rule (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior explicitly specifies a fixed invalid returned value case.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"); call classify() and assert module returns "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly local normalization determinism for identical input; this is single-function logic with no required module boundary or I/O (MOCK-MISS => Unit). REAL-INFRA no, LLM-DEP no, MULTI-STEP no. Rejected higher level: behavior text narrows scope to normalization step only.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants already fixed in input) and assert identical output label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live provider calls and checks consistency of real model-returned labels. Mocking would hide model-side variability (MOCK-HIDE yes, LLM-DEP yes). Not Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline. Rejected System-only: success criterion depends on model decision consistency, not just transport/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real classification calls for the same input/taxonomy, log raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label stability whenever provider raw response is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between budget policy, token estimation/truncation logic, and dispatch gate (cross-module handoff). In-process wiring with fake sender can verify 'enforced before send' without real OS/network (REAL-INFRA no). No dependence on model output correctness (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is pre-dispatch policy enforcement, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget; invoke classify() with oversized document; assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and verify outbound sender receives only compliant payload or no call when reject policy is set.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call because the measured variable is provider-reported token usage; stubs can mask tokenizer/accounting mismatches (MOCK-HIDE yes). It traverses a real AI API call path (LLM-DEP default Agentic applies). Not Workflow: single call/step measurement. Rejected Integration: behavior text depends on provider-reported live usage, not just local budgeting code.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification on representative document(s), capture provider usage metadata, and assert reported input/total tokens are <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across client wrapper, backoff/wait policy, and response handling. Deterministic fake API sequence (rate-limit then success) exposes this without real network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no). Behavior tests infrastructure around call, not model decision quality (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text targets control-flow on error signal, not live model output.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client scripted to return rate-limit signal first and success second; assert module delays per policy, retries once (or configured count), and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
